methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $142M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Lumafield is a Series C robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $142M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of nine same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies, comprising eight private firms and one public comparable. Key private peers include Hadrian ($1.6B), 1X Technologies ($10.0B), Exotec ($2.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), and Dexterity ($1.6B), with the peer range spanning $300M to $10.0B.

Two triangulation methods are applied. Stage-progression analysis uses total raised ($142M) as a valuation proxy and applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C advancement, implying a valuation of $241M. Peer-median analysis across the nine comparable companies yields a median valuation of $1.6B, with that cohort's range of $300M–$10.0B establishing ceiling and floor context.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $205M (low) to $1.8B (high), with a median of $921M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty in stage-relative positioning: stage-progression anchors conservatively to capital deployed, while peer-median reflects recent market pricing in a capital-intensive, outcome-dependent sector. Key uncertainties include the company's technical maturity relative to later-stage peers, customer traction breadth, and path to near-term revenue. Clarification of Lumafield's product-market fit and manufacturing partnerships would be required to narrow this range.
